A big green space guy shoots aliens because they touched a giant hula hoop in space. The theme song gives me goosebumps every time.
I pay a monthly subscription to click on boars in a forest for hours until I level up. I haven't seen the sun since 2004.
I spent $500 trying to get an anime girl with a sword, but all I got were some 3-star spears. My wallet is crying.
Escorted a grumpy teenager across a post-apocalyptic wasteland. Cried in the first 15 minutes. Now I have emotional trauma.
I held an angle for three minutes, looked away to sneeze, and instantly got shot in the head. Toxic voice chat adds to the immersion.
Literally too angry to die. The soundtrack is just heavy metal playing while I rip and tear demons apart with my bare hands.
Thought it was just 2D block building, then a giant floating eye attacked me at night. Now I'm fighting a wall of flesh in the underworld.
An angry bald dad takes his son on a road trip to spread ashes, but ends up fighting every mythological creature along the way. 'BOY!'
Spent three minutes reinforcing walls and setting traps, only for the enemy to blow up the ceiling and drop right on my head.
I tapped the screen, hit a green pipe immediately, threw my phone at the wall, and cracked the screen. I miss this game.
Memorized a 14-button combo just to rip my opponent's spine out at the end of the match. Very family-friendly.
I have to stare at security cameras and close doors so creepy animatronics don't jump scare me. I am terrified.
Trying to fix generators while a masked killer chases my friends around a pallet. I missed one skill check and everyone died.
I was supposed to be an honorable samurai, but sneaking in the tall grass and throwing poison darts at people is just way more effective.
A beautiful 1930s cartoon where a singing flower and a giant frog absolutely destroyed my self-esteem for three hours straight.
